Details: One study suggests solar ultraviolet (UV) irradiation and hydrogen peroxide with iron as catalyst (i.e. photo-Fenton process) can be effective for oxamyl degradation. Oxamyl removal efficiency increases with increasing UV contact time while hydrogen peroxide and iron concentrations are fixed. 82% degradation was reached in approximately 23 minutes with 200 - 500 mg/L hydrogen peroxide and 20 mg/L iron [1517]. The pH level was in the range of 2.7 to 2.9 to avoid iron hydroxide precipitation [1517].
